<!-- PR TITLE: Use third person present tense with the PR as implicit subject.
     "Adds feature X", "Fixes bug Y" — not imperative "Add", "Fix". -->

<!-- TL;DR: Use a setext underline (-----), NOT an ATX ## heading.
     Write one prose paragraph — no bullets, no lists, no line breaks.
     Active voice throughout. 50-150 words. Summarize the full scope of
     the change so a reviewer can decide whether to read on. -->
TL;DR
-----



<!-- CLOSES: Place the issue reference between TL;DR and Details.
     Use "Closes" (not "Fixes" or "Resolves") for consistency. -->
Closes #

<!-- DETAILS: Use a setext underline (-------), NOT an ATX ## heading.
     The first sentence must directly state what the code does — lead
     with action, not background or problem context.

     Write in narrative paragraphs. No bullet lists. Active voice only —
     never passive ("was deployed", "is rejected"). Never refer to the
     PR or changes as an explicit subject ("This PR adds...",
     "The fix removes..."). Just state what happens.

     Cover design decisions, technical details, and implications. Each
     paragraph should advance the reader's understanding of a distinct
     aspect of the change. -->
Details
-------



<!-- TEST PLAN: This is the one section that uses an ATX ## heading.
     Each item should use - [x] (verified), never - [ ] (unchecked).
     PRs describe what was tested and the result, not what will be tested.
     Include concrete evidence: command output, record counts, HTTP
     status codes, test suite pass counts. -->
## Test plan

- [x]
